sections.paragraph: m. (it is not proved from Isa 48:4, to be also f.), with suff. מִצְחִי forehead, 1Sa 17:49. מֵצַח אִשָּׁה זֹונָה “the (impudent) forehead of a harlot,” Jer 3:3 Eze 3:7, חִזִקֵי מֵצַח “of an impudent forehead;” verses Eze 3:8, Eze 3:9 Isa 48:4, מִצְחֲךָ נְחוּשָׁה “thy forehead (is) brass,” i.e. a brazen forehead.
